summary: Lewis Hamilton's arrival injects fresh momentum into Ferrari's championship assault as the Scuderia seeks to capitalize on the British driver's confidence-building victory in Spain. McLaren's constructors' title ambitions collide with Red Bull's defensive strategy at a track where aerodynamic efficiency determines grid position and race pace. A strong Ferrari result at Spa would signal whether Hamilton's presence has genuinely stabilized the team's confidence or merely masked deeper structural vulnerabilities ahead of the season's critical final stretch.
